draft.introduction: The Vivekachūḍāmaṇi by Śaṅkara is the classic manual of Advaita Vedānta. It teaches that the Self is Brahman and all else is superimposition (adhyāsa).

translation.text: Hard to obtain is human birth, harder still the desire for liberation, and hardest of all is the company of the wise. Through the grace of God alone does one obtain these three.

Brahman alone is real; the world is appearance. The individual Self is none other than Brahman. Like the space within a pot and the space outside, the Self within the body and the Self in all things are one and the same.

translator_notes.note: Śaṅkara immediately subordinates human birth to the desire for liberation (mumuḥkṣutva)—without the right aspiration, even human birth is wasted. This is spiritual elitism of the most rigorous kind.
